REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, RESPONDENTS SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS, SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING DEPARTMENT AND SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS SUSTAINED. PETITIONER'S AMENDED PETITION FAILED TO STATE FACTS SHOWING ANY ACT THE RESPONDENTS ARE COMPELLED TO PERFORM IN RELATION TO THE RESPONDENTS' MARCH 15, 2022, DECISION APPROVING MJ MISSION DOLORES' APPLICATION TO BUILD A 5-STORY BUILDING. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE AMENDED PETITION ALLEGES THAT THE MARCH 15, 2022, DECISION PREVENTED THE RESPONDENTS FROM ISSUING THE JUNE 27, 2023, DECISION, THE RESPONDENTS' DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TIME-BARRED BY GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 65009(C)(1)(E) AND SECTION 65009(E). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Joinder In Demurrer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Brian F. Crossman; Deputy City Attorney; 415-554-4690; appearing as counsel for Respondents San Francisco Board of Supervisors, San Francisco Planning Department and San Francisco Planning Commission.
Brian J. O'Neill; Patterson & O'Neill, PC; 415-907-9110; appearing as counsel for Real Parties in Interest MJ Mission Dolores, LLC.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

MJ MISSION DOLORES LLC'S NOTICE OF JOINDER IN DEMURRER is SUSTAINED. Petitioner's Amended Petition failed to state facts showing any act the Respondents are compelled to perform in relation to the Respondents' March 15, 2022, decision approving MJ Mission Dolores' application to build a 5-story building. To the extent that the Amended Petition alleges that the March 15, 2022, decision prevented the Respondents from issuing the June 27, 2023, decision, the Respondents' Demurrer is sustained WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as time-barred by Government Code section 65009(c)(1)(E) and section 65009(e).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Apr-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Brian F. Crossman; Deputy City Attorney; 415-554-4690; appearing as counsel for Respondents San Francisco Board of Supervisors, San Francisco Planning Department and San Francisco Planning Commission.
Brian J. O'Neill; Patterson & O'Neill, PC; 415-907-9110; appearing as counsel for Real Parties in Interest MJ Mission Dolores, LLC.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENTS SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS, SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING DEPARTMENT AND SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is SUSTAINED. Petitioner's Amended Petition failed to state facts showing any act the Respondents are compelled to perform in relation to the Respondents' March 15, 2022, decision approving MJ Mission Dolores' application to build a 5-story building. To the extent that the Amended Petition alleges that the March 15, 2022, decision prevented the Respondents from issuing the June 27, 2023, decision, the Respondents' Demurrer is sustained WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as time-barred by Government Code section 65009(c)(1)(E) and section 65009(e).

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
